channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Unwanted guns become tools and jewelry
item.description: A group in North Carolina helps people turn unwanted guns into tools and jewelry. For some, it's a healing process.
item.content:encoded: A group in North Carolina helps people turn unwanted guns into tools and jewelry. For some, it's a healing process.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Williams

item.title: A 15-year-old shoplifter changed his course when another teen whispered a message
item.description: As a teenager, Everett Miller went through a rough time and started shoplifting. He recalls how a girl's whispered message kept him from making a terrible mistake.
item.content:encoded: As a teenager, Everett Miller went through a rough time and started shoplifting. He recalls how a girl's whispered message kept him from making a terrible mistake.

item.title: The 11 most challenged books of 2025, according to the American Library Association
item.description: The ALA says 4,235 titles were challenged at U.S. libraries — the second-highest year on record. Forty percent of the challenged works involved LGBTQ+ subjects or the experiences of people of color.
item.content:encoded:
Sold by Patricia McCormick, The Perks of Being a Wallflower by Stephen Chbosky and Maia Kobabe's Gender Queer: A Memoir.'/>

The ALA says 4,235 titles were challenged at U.S. libraries — the second-highest year on record. Forty percent of the challenged works involved LGBTQ+ subjects or the experiences of people of color.

(Image credit: American Library Association)
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Tsioulcas

item.title: Malala Yousafzai on life before and after being shot by a Taliban gunman
item.description: As a teen, Yousafzai risked her life speaking out against the Taliban. "At the time, what scared me more was a life without an education as a girl," the Nobel-winner told Terry Gross at a live event.
item.content:encoded: As a teen, Yousafzai risked her life speaking out against the Taliban. "At the time, what scared me more was a life without an education as a girl," the Nobel-winner told Terry Gross at a live event.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross

item.title: The Sonoran Desert teems with wildlife. These 3D scans could help protect its future
item.description: A new art exhibit in Phoenix features some of the world's prickliest plants. It could also help save them.
item.content:encoded:
A new art exhibit in Phoenix features some of the world's prickliest plants. It could also help save them.

(Image credit: Caitlin O'Hara for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Alina Hartounian

item.title: Sunday Puzzle: For Mimi
item.description: NPR's Elissa Nadworny plays the puzzle with WMEH listener Chee Sing Lee and Weekend Edition Puzzlemaster Will Shortz.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Elissa Nadworny plays the puzzle with WMEH listener Chee Sing Lee and Weekend Edition Puzzlemaster Will Shortz.
item.dc:creator: Will Shortz

item.title: Friendships and flower arrangements: Keeping the Japanese art of ikebana alive
item.description: Ikebana is the Japanese art of floral arranging. Ikebana International is introducing Americans to the art and promoting friendship through flowers.
item.content:encoded: Ikebana is the Japanese art of floral arranging. Ikebana International is introducing Americans to the art and promoting friendship through flowers.
item.dc:creator: Margaux Bauerlein

item.title: Sunday Puzzle: For Mimi
item.description: NPR's Elissa Nadworny plays the puzzle with WMEH listener Chee Sing Lee and Weekend Edition Puzzlemaster Will Shortz.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Elissa Nadworny plays the puzzle with WMEH listener Chee Sing Lee and Weekend Edition Puzzlemaster Will Shortz.
item.dc:creator: Will Shortz

item.title: 'Wait Wait' for April 18. 2026: With Not My Job guest Phil Pritchard
item.description: This week, Wait Wait is live in Chicago with host Peter Sagal, guest judge and scorekeeper Alzo Slade, special guest Phil Pritchard and panelists Alonzo Bodden, Adam Burke, and Dulcé Sloan
item.content:encoded:
This week, Wait Wait is live in Chicago with host Peter Sagal, guest judge and scorekeeper Alzo Slade, special guest Phil Pritchard and panelists Alonzo Bodden, Adam Burke, and Dulcé Sloan

(Image credit: Bruce Bennett)

item.title: Photos: How overfishing in Southeast Asia is an ecological and human crisis
item.description: A rare look at one of the world's most critical and understudied environmental crises. Southeast Asia produces more than half of the world's fish, yet its waters are among the most depleted and contested.
item.content:encoded:
A rare look at one of the world's most critical and understudied environmental crises. Southeast Asia produces more than half of the world's fish, yet its waters are among the most depleted and contested.

(Image credit: Nicole Tung)
item.dc:creator: Nicole Tung
